description: PromptPal Phase 1 CLI — a bash+Python3 tool that improves prompts via Claude CLI or Anthropic API backends, with multi-turn refinement, local history, WSL2 support, and winget distribution.

userStories.title: Create project skeleton and Backend ABC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core directory structure and Backend abstract base class in place so all subsequent stories have a stable foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure exists: bin/, core/, tests/unit/, tests/integration/, defaults/
core/backend.py defines Backend ABC with name, complete(system, messages, stream), and check_auth() methods
core/__init__.py, core/config.py (Config dataclass with all fields from SPEC §4/§10) exist
defaults/config.json exists with all default field values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement Config loading with merge order
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Config to load defaults, then config.json overrides, so all components get correct runtime configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Config.load() merges: dataclass defaults → ~/.promptpal/config.json overrides → CLI flag overrides
Unknown fields in config.json are silently ignored
Type-mismatched fields fall back to default with a stderr warning
Corrupt config.json (JSON parse error) → exit 1 with 'Config file corrupt at ~/.promptpal/config.json. Delete it to reset.'
Config.preferred_backend accepts exactly 'auto' | 'claude-cli' | 'api-key'; other values fall back to 'auto' with stderr warning
Writes to config.json use atomic tempfile.mkstemp → os.rename pattern
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement core/platform.py — detect_platform and clipboard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want platform detection and clipboard support so the tool behaves correctly on macOS, Linux, and WSL2.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_platform() reads /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease; sets is_wsl=True when 'microsoft' found, wsl_version=2 when 'wsl2' found
HOME guard: if is_wsl=True and HOME starts with /mnt/c/ or /c/, print WSL launch fix message to stderr and exit 1
_resolve_home() cross-checks HOME against passwd entry; passwd wins when HOME is NTFS-mounted
Clipboard provider priority: xclip → xsel → pbcopy → clip.exe (WSL only) → none
clip.exe sends UTF-8 without BOM; verified for non-ASCII characters
When no clipboard provider is available, copy_to_clipboard() prints one-line warning to stderr and returns without error
All files written by PromptPal use LF line endings
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement ApiBackend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ApiBackend to call the Anthropic Messages API so users with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY can improve prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: ApiBackend implements Backend ABC against POST /v1/messages with anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
API key read only from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var; never written to disk, logs, or stdout
Retry: 429 honors Retry-After (max 3 retries); 5xx exponential backoff 1s/2s/4s (max 3); network errors retry once after 2s; 401 fails immediately
complete() returns full assistant text; streams tokens to terminal when stream=True and stdout is a TTY
check_auth() performs minimal messages call with max_tokens=1
Each turn records backend: 'api-key', input_tokens, output_tokens as numeric values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement CliBackend with stream-json pipe
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CliBackend to invoke the claude CLI via stream-json so multi-turn context is preserved natively without role-prefix hacks.
acceptanceCriteria: CliBackend invokes: claude --print --model <m> --bare --system-prompt-file <path> --input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json
messages array is fed as NDJSON on stdin; assistant/message events consumed from stdout
Auth failure detected via non-zero exit + auth keyword in stderr → 'Claude CLI auth failed. Run: claude auth login'
check_auth() runs claude --version
Each turn records backend: 'claude-cli', input_tokens: null, output_tokens: null
A prompt containing the literal substring 'Human:' produces a correct improved prompt (no role-prefix confusion)
3 successive iterations send all 5 prior messages entries as NDJSON on turn 3
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement resolve_backend and backend persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want backend resolution with explicit-flag persistence so users who pick a backend keep using it without re-specifying it each run.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_backend(preferred, model) selection order: explicit flag → Config.preferred_backend → auto-detect (claude on PATH → CliBackend; else ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set → ApiBackend; else NoBackendError)
When preferred == 'claude-cli' and claude missing → FileNotFoundError with actionable text; no fallback to API
When preferred == 'api-key' and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset → env-var instructions message; no fallback to CLI
When neither backend available → exit 1 with two-option setup message containing 'Option 1 (Claude CLI):' and 'Option 2 (API key):'
--backend <claude-cli|api-key> persists to config.preferred_backend after first successful turn only
--backend auto resets config.preferred_backend to 'auto' immediately
Failed call does not persist backend choice
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement system prompt management
userStories.description: As a developer, I want system prompt seeding and update logic so the bundled default is always available and updates are integrity-verified.
acceptanceCriteria: core/system_prompt.txt exists with a default prompt
On first run (no ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md), installer seeds from core/system_prompt.txt
~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md is never overwritten without explicit --update-system-prompt

--update-system-prompt downloads URL + .sha256 sidecar, verifies hash, atomically replaces ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md; on hash mismatch → exit 1 with 'checksum mismatch' message and existing file unchanged
Before atomic replace, current file is backed up to ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md.bak with path printed
--system-prompt FILE overrides resolved path for current invocation only (no persistence)
If system_prompt_path missing or unreadable → exit 1 with actionable message pointing to --update-system-prompt
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement history and persistence layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want session JSON files and an index so users can search, replay, and review past prompt refinements.
acceptanceCriteria: One JSON file per session at ~/.promptpal/history/<uuid>.json with schema per SPEC §4
~/.promptpal/history/index.json maintained with fields: session_id, created_at, label, status, original_prompt_preview
All writes use atomic tempfile.mkstemp → os.rename; temp file cleaned up on any write failure
Session file updated on every turn with status: 'in-progress'; finalized to 'accepted' or 'discarded' on loop exit
Append-only NDJSON usage.log with one line per turn; input_tokens/output_tokens may be null
Config.max_history_entries enforced by evicting oldest entries from index and disk after every accept/discard
History write OSError is non-fatal: warns to stderr, run continues, exit code unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement prompt input resolution and diff display
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pipeline to accept prompts from CLI args, stdin, or interactive TTY, and show a unified diff for long outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: promptpal "your prompt" reads prompt from positional arg
echo "prompt" | promptpal reads from stdin when stdin is not a TTY
When no arg and stdin is a TTY, prompt user for interactive input
Unified diff displayed between original and improved prompt when improved length > 3 lines
Final improved prompt written to stdout; all logs, diff chrome, spinner go to stderr
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement refinement loop (accept/iterate/discard/raw/copy)
userStories.description: As a user, I want an interactive refinement loop so I can accept, iterate on, or discard the improved prompt in a single session.
acceptanceCriteria: After displaying improved prompt, show choice line: [a]ccept [i]terate [d]iscard [r]aw [c]opy
[i]terate reads user feedback, appends as user turn, calls Backend.complete(), appends response, re-displays; full messages array sent every call
At minimum 3 successive iterations supported in a single session
[a]ccept writes session JSON with status: 'accepted', copies to clipboard if auto_copy or --copy, exits 0
[d]iscard writes session JSON with status: 'discarded', exits 0
[r]aw prints raw improved prompt to stderr (not stdout), re-presents choice line
[c]opy copies current improved prompt to clipboard, re-presents choice line; warns non-fatally if no provider
--iterations N runs N auto-iterations with synthesized feedback turn before presenting choice line
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement CLI argument parsing (core/cli.py)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all flags parsed via argparse into a CLIOptions dataclass so every feature has a clean programmatic interface.
acceptanceCriteria: core/cli.py uses argparse; all flags from PRD §5.4 are wired: --model, --iterations, --no-history, --copy, --show-history, --replay, --system-prompt, --output, --quiet, --search, --export, --name, --update-system-prompt, --uninstall, --backend, --status
--output accepts plain | json | markdown; json emits single object {original, improved, turns, session_id, backend, model}
--quiet suppresses diff/spinner/streaming/choice; emits only improved prompt on stdout; auto-accepts after first turn
--show-history prints paginated list from index.json newest-first then exits 0
--search KEYWORD matches index original_prompt_preview and label first; falls back to scanning session files; results sorted by created_at descending
--status prints backend, model, auth check, platform, config path, history count in < 20 lines; exits 0
--export SESSION_ID dumps full session JSON to stdout and exits 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement bin/promptpal bash entrypoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want a bash entrypoint that guards against WSL HOME regression and delegates to the Python core so the tool is a single command on PATH.
acceptanceCriteria: bin/promptpal is a bash script that delegates to python3 -m core.main
Entrypoint duplicates the WSL HOME guard (HOME starts with /mnt/c/ or /c/) and exits 1 with the WSL fix message before Python starts
shellcheck bin/promptpal returns clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement install.sh
userStories.description: As a user, I want install.sh to set up ~/.promptpal/ and place the binary on PATH so I can start using the tool immediately after running the one-liner.
acceptanceCriteria: install.sh works on macOS, Linux, and WSL2 Ubuntu without extra runtime installs beyond bash, python3, curl/wget
Installer refuses to run when HOME is /mnt/c/... or /c/...; prints wsl -d Ubuntu fix and exits 1
Binary placed at $INSTALL_DIR/promptpal (default ~/.local/bin/promptpal); warns if not on PATH
~/.promptpal/ created with history/, config.json (from defaults/config.json), and system-prompt.md (from core/system_prompt.txt); existing files never overwritten
Post-install backend check runs and prints which backend(s) are configured; if none, prints two-option setup hint (non-fatal, exits 0)
shellcheck install.sh returns clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement winget Windows launcher
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want a winget-distributed launcher so I can run promptpal from PowerShell without managing WSL manually.
acceptanceCriteria: launcher/promptpal.exe (or promptpal.bat / promptpal.ps1) checks for WSL Ubuntu; if absent, prints 'wsl --install -d Ubuntu' instruction and exits 1
When WSL Ubuntu is present, forwards all args via wsl -d Ubuntu -- promptpal "$@"
Launcher contains no Anthropic logic
winget manifest exists and passes winget validate
Given WSL Ubuntu installed and PromptPal installed inside it, running promptpal from PowerShell returns the improved prompt to PowerShell stdout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement uninstall.sh and --replay flag
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cleanly uninstall the tool and replay past sessions so I can maintain my environment and continue refining saved prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: uninstall.sh removes the installed binary
uninstall.sh prompts (Yn) before removing ~/.promptpal/; --purge removes without prompting
--replay SESSION_ID loads specified session, replays messages into a new session, enters refinement loop
--name LABEL assigns human-readable label recorded in session file and index
shellcheck uninstall.sh returns clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire full test suite and CI matrix
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the complete test suite passing at ≥80% coverage across all required test files so the codebase is releasable.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/unit/test_backend.py covers all resolve_backend paths (P1-BKND-01..09)
tests/unit/test_cli_backend_streamjson.py verifies stdin NDJSON, command vector with --bare --system-prompt-file --input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json
tests/unit/test_backend_persistence.py covers --backend api-key persists, --backend auto clears, failed call does not persist
tests/unit/test_system_prompt.py covers bundled-default seed, no-overwrite on existing, sidecar verify, mismatch refusal
tests/unit/test_platform.py covers WSL detection, HOME guard, clipboard priority, UTF-8 round-trip
tests/unit/test_history.py covers atomic write, index upsert, eviction, search, incremental update
tests/unit/test_config.py covers defaults, file override, CLI override, corrupt file, unknown-field tolerance
tests/unit/test_diff.py covers short prompt → no diff; long prompt → unified diff
tests/integration/test_stdin.py covers arg, stdin, and interactive input paths
tests/integration/test_pipeline.py covers all loop branches with mock backend
tests/integration/test_flags.py covers one assert per flag
pytest --cov=core reports ≥ 80% coverage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
